question_title: How much will "Michael" gross domestically during the film's opening weekend?
summary: The "Michael" biopic opening weekend forecast centers on $76M, driven by a tension between two camps of industry trackers. The bullish camp (Box Office Pro, World of Reel) projects $80-90M based on unprecedented pre-sale velocity, while the conservative camp (Deadline tracking at $60M+, BoxOfficeTheory at $52-75M) anchors more on genre precedent. No music biopic has ever opened above $60.2M domestically, but Michael's pre-sale data, record-breaking trailer views (116.2M in 24 hours), and first-choice tracking ahead of Bohemian Rhapsody and Oppenheimer at the same point suggest this ceiling will be shattered. The major unknown is reviews — the world premiere is April 10, three days after this question closes, creating significant symmetric risk. The distribution is modeled as a scenario mixture: 20% conservative ($58M center, reviews disappoint), 50% base case ($75M, decent reviews), 30% bullish ($90M, great reviews/cultural event).

factors.description: Box Office Pro (March 27) projects $80-90M opening based on pre-sale velocity described as 'absolutely killing it' — internal tracking data is the basis for highest estimates
Deadline 3-week tracking (April 2) indicates $60M+ floor with advance sales 'north of $50M' — most credible industry tracking source but typically conservative for event films with strong pre-sales
BoxOfficeTheory (March 27) projects $52-75M range, noting first-day general show sales behind blockbusters like Wicked — more conservative analytical approach

Record-breaking trailer views (116.2M in 24 hours) and first-choice tracking well ahead of Bohemian Rhapsody, Elvis, and Oppenheimer at same point — unprecedented audience interest signals for any biopic
No music biopic has ever opened above $60.2M domestically (Straight Outta Compton 2015) — genre ceiling creates structural uncertainty about whether demand can actually reach $80M+

Reviews unknown — world premiere April 10 in Berlin, 2 weeks before release. Positive reviews could push toward $85M+, negative reviews could cap at $55-65M. Exhibitors who have seen the film give 'very positive' reactions
Child abuse controversy and Leaving Neverland documentary (2019) could dampen turnout among some demographics, though pre-sales suggest it hasn't hurt demand so far
Historical pattern: music biopics with strong pre-sales tend to overperform tracking (Bohemian Rhapsody and Straight Outta Compton both significantly exceeded early tracking estimates)

reasoning:
**Forecast: "Michael" Opening Weekend Domestic Gross**

**Median estimate: $76.2M** (80% CI: $57.8M - $93.6M)

Key findings from my research:

- **Bullish signal ($80-90M):** Box Office Pro and internal tracking show pre-sales "absolutely killing it," with first-choice metrics ahead of Bohemian Rhapsody, Elvis, and Oppenheimer at the same tracking point
- **Conservative signal ($55-75M):** Deadline's 3-week tracking says "$60M+" (typically a conservative floor), while BoxOfficeTheory projects $52-75M noting general show first-day sales lag blockbusters
- **Genre precedent:** No music biopic has ever opened above $60.2M (Straight Outta Compton, 2015) — but unprecedented trailer views (116.2M in 24 hours) and pre-sale data suggest this ceiling will break
- **Major unknown:** Reviews drop after the April 10 Berlin premiere — this could swing the result ±$15M but occurs after the question closes

The distribution is modeled as a three-scenario mixture weighted by the probability of review outcomes and demand conversion, producing a right-skewed shape centered around $76M.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the gross domestic box office revenue that [*Michael*](https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt11378946/?ref_=bo_se_r_1) earns during its opening weekend, according to *Box Office Mojo*.

However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled.
